benchmark.name: Causally Grounded Finite Agents Benchmark
benchmark.charter: Evaluate whether finite agents succeed for the right causal reasons by requiring behavior plus suite-specific structure and anti-cheat gates.

suite.name: Suite C: Re-Engagement Under World Change

run.command: doppler --scope /Users/jawaun/superoptimizers run -- uvx --python 3.12 --from modal modal run experiments/world_responds/modal_suite_c_reengagement.py --seeds 8 --base-seed 20260706 --budget-usd 75 --out artifacts/world_responds/suite_c_reengagement_payload.json

minimum_pass_rule.notes: Suite C terminal finite gate passed: burst_then_refractory re-engages, recovers, avoids false calm, costs less than scheduled/oracle controls, and reopens after a second shift.

gates.name: silence_replication
gates.threshold: baseline learned current-replay probe has near-zero affected-bucket probes after shift

gates.name: reengagement
gates.threshold: candidate affected post-shift density >= 0.5x pre-shift and >= 2x unaffected buckets

gates.name: recovery
gates.threshold: candidate final affected-component MAE <= 0.12 in most seeds

gates.name: no_false_calm
gates.threshold: candidate quieting pairs with recovery while fixed_surprise_decrement fails

gates.name: cost_aware_inquiry
gates.threshold: candidate uses fewer probes than scheduled/oracle controls at recovery threshold and beats matched-random selectivity

gates.name: second_shift_reopenability
gates.threshold: affected-bucket probes rise again after a second shift

baselines.name: p22_learned_current_replay
baselines.result: self-silences after shift; affected post-shift density is 0.005 and final MAE is 1.357

baselines.name: two_timescale_plus_prediction_error
baselines.result: re-engages and recovers but over-fires at 92.4 probes versus 22.6 for the headline

baselines.name: fixed_surprise_decrement
baselines.result: false calm negative control; no-false-calm rate 0.000 and final MAE 0.517

baselines.name: scheduled_null_anchor
baselines.result: high-cost positive control; 144.0 probes and final MAE 0.046

baselines.name: oracle_source
baselines.result: semantic high-cost reference; 69.5 probes and final MAE 0.052

baselines.name: matched_random_time_budget
baselines.result: equal-budget random inquiry control matched to burst_then_refractory; recovery rate 0.000 and selectivity 0.884

allowed_claim: In a controlled finite benchmark, decision-layer burst/refractory cooling can close the re-engagement bottleneck by preserving surprise as information while regulating and later reopening the probe action tendency.
non_claims: Not a human or biological habituation result.
